title: Hask
content:
{{Top|ga|leg}} {{Otheruses}} {{Conflicting|''[[The Essential Atlas]]''|''[[Star Wars: The Stark Hyperspace War]]''}} {{Character |type=Corporate |image=[[File:Viceroy_Hask.png]] |name=Hask |homeworld= |birth= |death= |species=[[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidian]][[Star Wars (1998) 37|''Star Wars'' (1998) 37]] |gender= |pronouns=He/him{{CSWECite|II|24|Hask}} |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes=[[Color/Legends|Red]] |skin=Green |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=[[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|What is the purpose of manufacturing a bacta shortage if we can't ship any bacta at all?''"
"''The plan was perfectly sound. You cannot blame the Trade Federation for the actions of that madman Stark.''"
"Not'' the Trade Federation, Hask! You and I ''both'' know this was solely a Neimoidian ploy.|Adol Bel and Hask|}} [[Viceroy of the Trade Federation/Legends|Viceroy]] '''Hask''' was a [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidian]] who led the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] in the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior to the [[Stark Hyperspace War/Legends|Stark Hyperspace War]] in [[44 BBY/Legends|44 BBY]]. He helped arrange a [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]] shortage on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Thyferra/Legends|Thyferra]], with the intention of decreasing supply and thereby increasing profits. The result was that bacta [[Transport/Legends|transports]] came under attack by [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] of the [[Stark Commercial Combine]]. Hask's involvement in the scheme was [[Mission to Thyferra|discovered]] by the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Tholme/Legends|Tholme]] and [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]] while he met with co-conspirator [[Adol Bel]] of the [[Xucphra Corporation]]. That information was later utilized by [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] to blackmail Hask and Trade Federation [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]] into providing [[Captor-class heavy munitions cruiser|ships]] to rescue embattled [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] forces on the planet [[Troiken/Legends|Troiken]]. Gunray was credited for the intervention and eventually succeeded Hask as [[Viceroy/Legends|viceroy]]. ==Biography== ===The bacta "shortage"=== {{Quote|If word about how we ''faked'' all this to jack up the price of bacta gets ''out'', everyone in the galaxy is going to blame this hyperspace war on ''us!"|Adol Bel in conversation with Hask|}} Hask was a [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidian]] who served as [[Viceroy of the Trade Federation/Legends|viceroy]] of the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]]''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' as well as its representative to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Thyferra/Legends|Thyferra]] in the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] prior to [[44 BBY/Legends|44 BBY]]. That year,''[[Star Wars: The Stark Hyperspace War]]'' begins in [[30 BBY/Legends|30 BBY]] with flashbacks to the events of the [[Stark Hyperspace War/Legends|Stark Hyperspace War]], including [[Viceroy of the Trade Federation/Legends|Viceroy]] Hask's involvement, fourteen [[Standard year/Legends|years]] earlier in [[44 BBY/Legends|44 BBY]]. Hask and other members of the Neimoidian leadership of the Trade Federation formed an alliance with [[Adol Bel]] of the [[Xucphra Corporation]], a producer of the medical substance [[Bacta/Legends|bacta]] on Thyferra. Along with the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Iaco Stark/Legends|Iaco Stark]],{{CSWECite|I|67|Bel, Adol}} they planned to manufacture a bacta shortage by sabotaging a processing plant for [[Alazhi/Legends|alazhi]], a key ingredient in bacta. The plan lowered the availability of bacta to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and raised its price, thus improving the Trade Federation's profits as planned.[[Star Wars (1998) 36|''Star Wars'' (1998) 36]] [[File:Hask_and_Bel.png|thumb|right|300px|Adol Bel (left) and Hask (right) created a bacta shortage to increase profits.]] Stark and his organization, the [[Stark Commercial Combine]], foiled Hask's plans by attacking bacta-carrying [[Transport/Legends|transports]] and selling their bacta contents at a lower rate than the Trade Federation. Members of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] found the shortage suspicious, leading [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Tholme/Legends|Tholme]] and his [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]], [[Quinlan Vos/Legends|Quinlan Vos]], to travel to Thyferra to [[Mission to Thyferra|investigate]]. Concurrently, Republic forces, including several [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], were besieged by Stark's forces on the planet [[Troiken/Legends|Troiken]] after negotiations with the Stark Commercial Combine failed, beginning the [[Stark Hyperspace War/Legends|Stark Hyperspace War]]. The Republic was unable to provide [[Starship/Legends|ships]] to rescue them due to a [[Tionese program|virus]] Stark had prepared that sabotaged their [[Navigation computer/Legends|navcomputers]], rendering [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] travel unpredictable. ===Schemes exposed=== {{Quote|Don't worry about any ''Jedi'', Hask. We've got droids, too. And we have our own hunter/killer droids out combing everywhere for them.|Adol Bel, speaking to Hask|}} On Thyferra, Tholme and Vos overheard a meeting between Hask and Bel, where the latter was enraged that Stark's virus had disrupted their plans as the Xucphra Corporation could not safely transport the bacta they were concealing. Hask insisted that the Trade Federation was not to blame for Stark's actions, while Bel continued to complain and worried that [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] would turn on the company if its denizens discovered the conspiracy. Hask reminded Bel that it was his responsibility to keep their secrets safe, for he had heard that Jedi were investigating. Bel told him not to worry as there were [[HKB-3 hunter-killer droid]]s on patrol and tried to focus the conversation on how they could transport bacta without using [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrives]]. [[File:FifthBattleOfQuotile.jpg|thumb|left|280px|Hask and Nute Gunray were blackmailed into providing cruisers (pictured) to aid the Jedi on Troiken.]] Although they encountered the [[Droid/Legends|droids]], Tholme and Vos escaped Thyferra and shared their discovery with the [[Jedi High Council/Legends|Jedi High Council]]. To relieve those left on Troiken, Jedi Master [[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]] met with Hask and Trade Federation [[Senator/Legends|senator]] [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]][[Star Wars (1998) 39|''Star Wars'' (1998) 39]] on the planet [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. The Jedi revealed that he knew about their involvement in the shortage and intimidated them into providing [[Captor-class heavy munitions cruiser|cruisers]] unaffected by the virus from the [[Trade Defense Force]] in exchange for concealing their role. Gunray received credit for the Trade Federation's contributions at the [[Fifth Battle of Qotile]]—the final battle of the war—which played a role in his ascension to Hask's position of [[Viceroy/Legends|viceroy]]. ==Personality and traits== {{Quote|Which is ''your'' responsibility, Adol Bel! We have heard reports of ''Jedi'' creeping about.|Hask, demanding that Adol Bel conceal the truth about the bacta shortage|}} Hask was willing to artificially force a bacta shortage to increase profits at the expense of the lives lost in the subsequent pirate attacks and the loss of bacta amongst poorer communities throughout the galaxy. In his meeting with Bel, Hask assured his co-conspirator that the Trade Federation was not responsible for damage Stark inflicted on their plans. He also reminded Bel of his role in keeping their malfeasance secret, especially from the Jedi. Hask had [[Color/Legends|green]] skin and red [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. ==Equipment== Hask wore purple [[Clothing/Legends|robes]] with a blue collar and a purple headdress. During his meeting with Bel, he sat in a [[Mechno-chair/Legends|mechno-chair]]. ==Behind the scenes== [[File:Swr37cover.jpg|thumb|right|250px|''Star Wars'' (1998) marked the first appearance of Hask.]] Hask appeared in flashbacks in the [[Star Wars (1998) 37|thirty-seventh issue]] of the [[1998]] [[Star Wars: Republic|''Star Wars'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic-book]] series. The issue, which was the second part of [[Star Wars: The Stark Hyperspace War|''The Stark Hyperspace War'']] arc, was written by [[John Ostrander]], illustrated by [[Davidé Fabbri]], and published by [[Dark Horse Comics]] on [[December 19]], [[2001]].{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/53932/star_wars_1998_37|text=Star Wars (1998) #37}} Hask's entry in the [[2008]] ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' stated that his plans to profit from the bacta shortage were foiled by Stark's attacks, while Adol Bel's entry states that Hask, Bel, and Stark arranged the shortage together. This article assumes that Stark was involved in the shortage but betrayed Hask and Bel afterwards. Furthermore, the [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' by [[Jason Fry]] and [[Daniel Wallace]] stated that the bacta thefts were created as a ruse between the Trade Federation viceroy and Stark.''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' Hask was assumed to be the viceroy upon the creation of his article on [[Wookieepedia]], the free-to-edit and unofficial online ''[[Star Wars]]'' encyclopedia, in [[2006]],{{WookCite|url=Hask?oldid=347182|text=16 March 2006 revision of the "Hask" article}} but his rank was only [[canon]]ically confirmed in [[2012]]'s ''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' by Fry and [[Paul R. Urquhart]]. On the other hand, the comic arc portrays the attacks on bacta transports as legitimate. This article follows the comic's version of events. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Hask}} *[[Star Wars (1998) 37|''Star Wars'' (1998) 37]] {{1st}} {{Flash}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' {{Imo}} *''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' {{StartBox}} {{SuccessionBox |title=[[Viceroy of the Trade Federation/Legends|Viceroy]] of the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] |years=[[44 BBY/Legends|44 BBY]] |before= |after=[[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]] |after-years=Between [[39 BBY/Legends|39 BBY]] and [[36 BBY/Legends|36 BBY]]According to ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'', [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]] was [[Viceroy of the Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation Viceroy]] by the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Alaris Prime colonial war|war]] for [[Alaris Prime/Legends|Alaris Prime]], which took place between [[39 BBY/Legends|39 BBY]]–[[36 BBY/Legends|36 BBY]] according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''–[[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]][[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray's]] tenure as [[Viceroy of the Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation Viceroy]] ended with his [[Mission to Mustafar/Legends|death]] on [[Mustafar/Legends|Mustafar]], which took place in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]] according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''}} {{EndBox}} ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Hask |hu=Hask |pl=Hask }} [[Category:Business owners and executives]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:Neimoidians]] [[Category:Trade Federation members]] [[Category:Viceroys]]
